title: Inophloeum armatum
taxonomy: Inophloeum armatum is classified as a species of fungus in the family Ophiostomataceae. It belongs to the phylum Ascomycota and the kingdom Fungi. Inophloeum armatum is a type of resinous fungus that grows on trees, particularly in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The classification of Inophloeum armatum is still being researched and may be subject to change as new information becomes available. Further study is needed to confirm its exact classification within the fungal kingdom.

intro: Inophloeum armatum, also known as the "Malagasy wood tree," is a species of plant in the family Euphoriaceae, native to the tropical forests of Madagascar. The health benefits of Inophloeum armatum include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as skin infections and wounds.
